request.question: Replacing sucrose with trehalose as a cryoprotectant in the freezing medium will increase post-thaw viability of HeLa cells by at least 15 percentage points compared to the standard DMSO protocol, due to trehalose's superior membrane stabilization at low temperatures.

hypothesis.intervention: Replacing sucrose with trehalose as a cryoprotectant in the freezing medium
hypothesis.threshold: at least 15 percentage points higher than DMSO control
hypothesis.mechanistic_rationale: due to trehalose's superior membrane stabilization at low temperatures
hypothesis.readiness_rationale: All core fields are present and specific, making the hypothesis CRO-ready today.
hypothesis.literature_search_hint: trehalose sucrose cryoprotectant HeLa post-thaw viability
hypothesis.original_hypothesis: Replacing sucrose with trehalose as a cryoprotectant in the freezing medium will increase post-thaw viability of HeLa cells by at least 15 percentage points compared to the standard DMSO protocol, due to trehalose's superior membrane stabilization at low temperatures.

plan.objective: To determine the effect of trehalose as a cryoprotectant on post-thaw viability of HeLa cells compared to a standard DMSO protocol.
plan.experimental_design: HeLa cells will be cryopreserved using two different protocols: one with trehalose and one with DMSO. Each condition will be replicated three times to ensure statistical validity. Post-thaw viability will be assessed using trypan blue exclusion after a 30-minute recovery period at 37°C.
controls: HeLa cells cryopreserved using 10% DMSO in freezing medium.
HeLa cells without any cryoprotectant in freezing medium (fresh cells).

step_by_step_protocol.description:
DAY 0 (PREP) | 1 h | Reagent Preparation
Prepare 10% (w/v) trehalose solution by dissolving 10 g of trehalose (Sigma-Aldrich, T9531) in 100 mL of sterile phosphate-buffered saline (PBS) at room temperature. Filter-sterilize using a 0.22 µm filter (Millipore) and store at 4°C until use.
Equipment: Magnetic stirrer, 0.22 µm filter, Sterile 100 mL bottle
Reagents: 10% (w/v) trehalose in PBS
Expected outcome: Trehalose solution is sterile and clear, ready for use in cryopreservation.

DAY 0 (PREP) | 30 min | Reagent Preparation
Prepare 10% (v/v) DMSO solution by diluting 10 mL of DMSO (Sigma-Aldrich, D8418) in 90 mL of sterile PBS. Store at 4°C until use.
Equipment: Sterile 100 mL bottle
Reagents: 10% (v/v) DMSO in PBS
Expected outcome: DMSO solution is clear and ready for use in cryopreservation.
Safety: DMSO is a skin irritant; wear gloves and goggles.

DAY 0 (PREP) | 24 h | Cell Culture
Culture HeLa cells in DMEM (Gibco, 11965) supplemented with 10% fetal bovine serum (FBS) (Gibco, 26140) and 1% penicillin-streptomycin (Gibco, 15140) at 37°C in a 5% CO2 incubator until 80% confluence is reached.
Equipment: 5% CO2 incubator, T-75 culture flasks
Reagents: DMEM, 10% FBS, 1% penicillin-streptomycin
Expected outcome: HeLa cells reach 80% confluence, ready for cryopreservation.

DAY 1 | 30 min | Cell Harvesting
Detach HeLa cells using 0.25% trypsin-EDTA (Gibco, 25200) for 5 min at 37°C. Neutralize with complete DMEM and centrifuge at 300 × g for 5 min at room temperature. Discard supernatant and resuspend cells in 1 mL of either trehalose or DMSO solution.
Equipment: Centrifuge (Eppendorf 5424), Pipettes, Tubes
Reagents: 0.25% trypsin-EDTA, Complete DMEM
Expected outcome: Cells are successfully detached, pelleted, and resuspended in cryoprotectant solution.

DAY 1 | 1 h | Cryopreservation
Aliquot 1 mL of cell suspension (1 × 10^6 cells/mL) into cryovials (Nunc) containing either trehalose or DMSO solution. Place cryovials in a controlled-rate freezer (Planer Kryo 10) and cool at a rate of -1°C/min to -80°C.
Equipment: Controlled-rate freezer (Planer Kryo 10), Cryovials (Nunc)
Expected outcome: Cells are frozen at -80°C without ice crystal formation.

DAY 1 | 30 min | Storage
Transfer cryovials to liquid nitrogen storage for long-term preservation.
Equipment: Liquid nitrogen storage tank
Expected outcome: Cryovials are securely stored in liquid nitrogen.
Safety: Liquid nitrogen poses a frostbite risk; handle with gloves.

DAY 30 | 10 min | Thawing
Retrieve cryovials from liquid nitrogen and immediately thaw in a 37°C water bath for 2 min until just thawed. Transfer contents to a 15 mL tube containing 9 mL of complete DMEM to dilute the cryoprotectant.
Equipment: 37°C water bath, 15 mL tubes
Reagents: Complete DMEM
Expected outcome: Cells are thawed and diluted, ready for recovery.

DAY 30 | 30 min | Recovery
Incubate the diluted cells at 37°C for 30 min to allow recovery before viability assessment.
Equipment: 5% CO2 incubator
Expected outcome: Cells recover from cryopreservation, ready for viability assessment.

DAY 30 | 30 min | Viability Assessment
After recovery, mix 100 µL of cell suspension with 100 µL of 0.4% trypan blue solution (Gibco, 15250). Count viable cells using a hemocytometer under a light microscope (Olympus CX23).
Equipment: Hemocytometer (Olympus CX23), Pipettes
Reagents: 0.4% trypan blue solution
Expected outcome: Viability percentage is determined, with viable cells excluding trypan blue stain.

DAY 30 | 1 h | Data Analysis
Calculate post-thaw viability percentage for each condition. Use ANOVA to compare means between trehalose and DMSO groups, with a significance level set at p < 0.05.
Expected outcome: Statistical significance is determined, with post-thaw viability of trehalose being at least 15 percentage points higher than DMSO control.

DAY 30 | Varies | Replication
Repeat the entire experiment three times to ensure reproducibility of results and statistical validity.
Expected outcome: Data from three independent experiments are collected for robust statistical analysis.

assumptions: Trehalose will provide better membrane stabilization than DMSO during freezing and thawing processes.
HeLa cells will survive the cryopreservation process with both cryoprotectants, allowing for viability assessment.
The trypan blue exclusion method will accurately reflect cell viability post-thaw.
decision_criteria: Proceed if post-thaw viability with trehalose is ≥ 15 percentage points higher than DMSO control.
Proceed if viability of both conditions is above 70% to ensure cells are healthy for further experiments.
reproducibility_notes: Use the same lot of trehalose and DMSO for all experiments.
Maintain consistent cell passage number (≤ 10 passages) for all HeLa cells used.
Calibrate the controlled-rate freezer before each experiment to ensure consistent cooling rates.
feedback_incorporated:
PRIOR SCIENTIST CORRECTIONS FOR SIMILAR EXPERIMENTS:
A scientist has previously flagged these specific issues on similar comparative_cryopreservation experiments. You MUST implement each correction below in your output unless it directly contradicts the current hypothesis:
1. replicate gate in plan text
2. add 30-minute post-thaw recovery at 37°C before trypan blue counting
3. change one

validation.primary_endpoint: Post-thaw viability percentage at immediate and 24h checkpoints.
validation.success_threshold: at least 15 percentage points higher than DMSO control improvement over comparator in replicate-confirmed data.
failure_conditions: No meaningful viability gain in replicate-confirmed runs.
Intervention increases delayed viability loss at 24h.
validation.suggested_statistical_comparison: Two-group comparison against comparator using t-test or Mann-Whitney based on normality.
validation.minimum_replicates_or_design_note: Minimum three biological replicates before execution-level claim.

cro_ready_brief.objective: To determine the effect of trehalose as a cryoprotectant on post-thaw viability of HeLa cells compared to a standard DMSO protocol.
scope_of_work: Execute pilot-first HeLa cryopreservation comparison between DMSO baseline and trehalose arm.
Deliver replicate-confirmed viability analysis and mitigation traceability report.
cro_ready_brief.sample_count: At least 3 biological replicates per arm
cro_ready_brief.materials_responsibility: CRO to quote and confirm all materials before ordering.
questions_for_cro: Can you support controlled-rate freezing logs in each run?
Can you include replicate-level batch metadata in the final report?
